refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation, task scheduling, and resource utilization.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations and verify that each sub-FR contributes to meeting or exceeding the target.

selected_dp.selected_dp: Adaptive equipment control algorithms, Dynamic task scheduling system, Resource coordination framework

potential_sources: Task scheduling depends on equipment status feedback
Resource coordination requires integration with task dispatch
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with well-defined interfaces to minimize tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable telemetry data for adaptive control
Risk of scheduling delays impacting productivity
Coordination complexity may increase system overhead
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider interface standardization and buffering to mitigate coupling risks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level integration of adaptive control, scheduling, and resource coordination functionalities.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time equipment status and receives control commands.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Handles task assignments and scheduling updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between modules and external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of all DPs with minimal diffusion; central integration module.
Contains equipment operation but limited scope for scheduling and coordination.
Focuses on remote control, less relevant for scheduling/resource coordination.
Primarily safety-related, minimal containment of productivity DPs.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing productivity-related DPs in the NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and improves integration.
containment_risks: Potential complexity and size of NGHE Solution module may impact maintainability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting NGHE Solution into submodules if complexity grows; ensure clear interface definitions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly maps to selected DPs with logical decomposition and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Centralized NGHE Solution module may become a bottleneck if diffusion increases.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the best architectural boundary for these DPs.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed at system level via productivity metrics and interface testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to DPs and IM boundary with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor module complexity and consider submodule decomposition if needed.
Refine interface definitions to reduce coupling and diffusion.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments meet criteria with acceptable risks; ready to proceed to next design phase.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; monitor complexity and coupling in subsequent levels.

assumptions: Stakeholders agree on productivity metrics and targets.
open_risks: Integration complexity in NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Performance data from prototype adaptive control and scheduling systems.
Interface compliance and communication latency measurements.
